dsl_rule.translation_notes: Used sma_100 as the long-term trend-following indicator per the 100-period lookback parameter. Price move abnormality represented by returns crossing ±2% threshold (approximating >2 standard deviations). Regime shift detection uses ADX > 25 to confirm trend initiation. Exit on trend reversal when price crosses back below/above the 100-period MA. Stop loss set to 3% to account for transaction costs and volatility during regime shifts.

backtest_rule.reason: Used sma_100 as the long-term trend-following indicator per the 100-period lookback parameter. Price move abnormality represented by returns crossing ±2% threshold (approximating >2 standard deviations). Regime shift detection uses ADX > 25 to confirm trend initiation. Exit on trend reversal when price crosses back below/above the 100-period MA. Stop loss set to 3% to account for transaction costs and volatility during regime shifts.

source_rule_text: A trader should use longer-term trend-following indicators to reduce transaction costs and capture abnormally large price moves that occur during major market shifts, rather than relying on shorter-term indicators that generate more frequent signals.
